ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 represents cola soft drink as a category; no brand-specific Coca-Cola entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0KL covers cola-type soft drinks as a category. No brand-specific Coca-Cola code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents cola drink as a substance category; closest parent used as no brand-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Coca-Cola, equivalent to approximately 330 g, as commonly served in Singapore and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, carbonated, cola, regular — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for regular carbonated cola beverage matches Coca-Cola (regular) composition. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml for this beverage). Protein and fat are confirmed zero by formulation.

gi_source.title: Coca-Cola, regular — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney (Atkinson FS et al., 2021, Am J Clin Nutr)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 63 for regular Coca-Cola is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2021). International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021: a systematic review. Am J Clin Nutr. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 35.0) / 100 = 22.1, where carbohydrate per 330 g serving = 10.6 × 3.3 = 34.98 ≈ 35.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g); however, high sugar load may contribute to weight gain which indirectly elevates blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Coca-Cola is very low in sodium and poses minimal direct hypertensive risk, but its high free-sugar content (approximately 35 g per 330 ml can) may promote weight gain and metabolic dysfunction that can raise blood pressure over time; individuals with hypertension should limit regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free-sugar content and medium glycemic index (GI 63) producing a high glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 22)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Regular Coca-Cola delivers approximately 35 g of free sugars per 330 ml can with a glycemic load of approximately 22, causing rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular cola and substitute with sugar-free or diet variants under clinical guidance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free-sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides, hepatic lipogenesis, and increased cardiovascular risk with habitual consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as regular Coca-Cola is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and higher cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise intake and prefer water or unsweetened beverages.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coca-Cola (Regular) is a globally recognised carbonated cola soft drink manufactured by The Coca-Cola Company, originally created in the United States in 1886. It is produced by combining carbonated water with sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, caramel colour (Class IV), phosphoric acid, natural flavours, and caffeine. The beverage is characterised by its distinctive sweet, slightly acidic taste with a caramel-cola flavour profile and effervescent mouthfeel. It contains no protein, no fat, and no dietary fibre; its caloric content derives entirely from free sugars (approximately 10.6 g per 100 ml, or 35 g per standard 330 ml can). In Singapore, Coca-Cola is widely available across all food service channels including hawker centres, restaurants, and food delivery platforms such as field survey. It is commonly served chilled or over ice as an accompaniment to meals. From a clinical nutrition perspective, regular Coca-Cola is a high-concern beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its medium glycemic index (GI 63) and high glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 22), which causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation. Its high free-sugar content also poses moderate cardiovascular risk through promotion of hypertriglyceridaemia and visceral adiposity with habitual consumption. The beverage is ultra-processed, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is known in Chinese as 可口可乐 (Simplified) or 可口可樂 (Traditional), in Tamil as கோகா-கோலா, and in Hindi as कोका-कोला.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Coke?
What is the sugar content of Coca-Cola?
What is the GI of Coca-Cola?
How much sugar is in a 330ml can of Coke?
Can I drink Coke if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851) for regular carbonated cola, which is an exact match for Coca-Cola (regular) composition. GI value of 63 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and corroborated by Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: contains no protein — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174851); Coca-Cola contains no protein by formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: contains no fat — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; Coca-Cola contains no fat by formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: naturally zero as this is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage with no lipid components. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero as carbonated soft drinks contain no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal components. All vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through vitamin_b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e) are 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; Coca-Cola is not fortified and contains no meaningful micronutrient content. Glycemic_index set to 63 (medium GI) per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is medium (56–69 range). Glycemic_load_per_serving calculated as (63 × 35.0 g carbohydrate per 330 ml serving) / 100 = 22.1. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as Coca-Cola is a carbonated soft drink. Cuisine reclassified from 'Chinese' (input tag) to 'Global / International' as Coca-Cola is a globally manufactured American brand product served across all cuisines. The input cuisine_tags (Chinese, Seafood Dish, BBQ, Crab) reflect the restaurant context (The Seafood Market) rather than the product identity and have not been carried forward as food classification attributes. Halal status noted as true based on Coca-Cola's global halal certification in Singapore; kosher status marked false as Singapore-sold Coca-Cola does not carry kosher certification. FoodOn FOODON_03301589 and FoodEx2 AN0KL are closest_parent matches as no brand-specific ontology codes exist. SNOMED CT 226465004 is closest_parent. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source) rather than -1 as the source explicitly reports these values.

improvement_actions_taken: Reclassified food_group from 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' to reflect product category accurately.
Reclassified cuisine from 'Chinese' to 'Global / International' as Coca-Cola is an American-origin global brand.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes for protein_g, fat_g, and all zero micronutrient values per zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index to 63 (medium) per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category set to 'medium' per validator threshold (56–69).
Populated all mandatory multilingual local_names: Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Ensured all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values: closest_parent for all three ontology systems.
Documented all sentinel and zero values in notes field per g3 and g4 requirements.
Calculated glycemic_load_per_serving as 22.1 based on 330 ml serving size and 10.6 g/100g carbohydrate.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
